The deadline passed at 13:30 while I was still at 95% completion, meaning my earlier buffer calculation was fatally flawed and I have already missed the submission window.

desires: Recover the academic grade lost due to my fatal miscalculation of the buffer time before I spiral into self-doubt about my planning competence.
Assertively communicate with Professor Assignment immediately to explain the specific calculation error and mitigate the penalty, rather than letting silence imply negligence.
Stop over-analyzing whether the task was actually cancelled versus my own flawed estimation so I can finish the remaining 5% and submit.
Prevent this single failure from derailing my long-term trajectory by treating it as a data point for future risk assessment rather than a character flaw.
